name: Guardrails (Team)
description: Security, compliance and privacy controls for team AI-assisted development. Full seven-layer governance including automated hooks, MDM enforcement, and comprehensive regulatory coverage.

post_install_actions.description: Open CLAUDE.md and fill in Section 0 — your stack, test command, lint command, and key directories.
post_install_actions.why: CLAUDE.md Section 0 is blank by design. Claude Code needs your project context before it can apply the security rules correctly.

post_install_actions.description: Review .claude/settings.json and add your project's test, lint, and build commands to the allow list.
post_install_actions.why: The allow list ships with common defaults. Your specific commands will not be auto-approved until added.

post_install_actions.description: Run 'make setup' in your project root to make hook scripts executable and run the verification checklist.
post_install_actions.why: Hook scripts must be executable to run. make setup also verifies that all layers are active before your first session.

post_install_actions.description: Run 'make deploy-settings' to copy managed-settings.json to the Claude Code system path on this machine.
post_install_actions.why: Layer 1 (MDM enforcement) only takes effect when managed-settings.json is deployed to the system path. This must be done once per machine.

commands.name: /security-review
commands.description: Run an OWASP checklist review of recent changes.
